title: 10 best herbal tinctures for oily face

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander tinctures helps with oily face because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to reduce excess sebum production and purify the skin. It also possesses antibacterial compounds that combat acne-causing bacteria, allowing for clearer and more balanced skin. Additionally, germander's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and congestion associated with oily complexions. As a result, regular use of herbal germander tinctures can help to achieve a healthier, more radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Germander's antioxidant properties help combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, leading to a reduction in sebum production and improvement in acne-prone skin.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Germander's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with oily skin, while also preventing further damage caused by environmental factors.

Antimicrobial activity: Germander's antimicrobial properties can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that contributes to acne and oily skin, reducing the growth of this bacteria and subsequent inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that have astringent and antibacterial properties, making them effective in controlling excess sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest teucrium chamaedrys leaves in the summer months and dry them thoroughly to reduce moisture content.
Chop the dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine one part chopped teucrium chamaedrys with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, leaving a 1-inch headspace.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking it daily, to allow for thorough extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture with ingredients and preparation date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with oily face because its antiseptic and astringent properties help to dry out pimples and reduce excess oil production. The herb's natural antibacterial compounds also combat acne-causing bacteria, preventing breakouts from forming in the first place. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory properties soothe redness and irritation, leaving skin looking calm and balanced. Regular use of sage tincture can help regulate sebum production, leading to a more even-toned and radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage tincture's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation and oxidative stress, leading to reduced sebum production and a balanced complexion.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in sage tincture help tighten pores, reduce oil secretion, and dry up acne-causing bacteria, resulting in a more even skin tone and reduced shine.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory agents reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, rosacea, and other skin irritations, promoting a clearer and healthier-looking complexion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have natural astringent and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and acne associated with oily skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the sage plant contain salvenic acids that help regulate sebum production, making them an excellent choice for tinctures targeting oily face concerns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanly dry harvested leaves in a warm place for 24-48 hours.
Use a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to finely chop 20 grams of dried leaves into small pieces.
Mix chopped leaves with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a ratio of 1:5 leaf-to-vodka and steep for 6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter after 6 weeks and store tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel tinctures helps with oily face because of its astringent and antiseptic properties. The herb's natural ability to shrink pores and reduce sebum production makes it an effective remedy for controlling oiliness and acne-prone skin. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of witch hazel help to combat bacterial growth that can exacerbate oily skin. By balancing the skin's natural pH levels and reducing inflammation, American witch hazel tinctures provide a gentle yet powerful way to regulate oily skin and promote a healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Witch Hazel tincture helps to reduce oil production and tighten pores, making it an effective natural remedy for controlling acne and minimizing shine.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Witch Hazel tincture soothe and calm redness and irritation caused by oily skin, reducing the appearance of blemishes and acne scars.

Antioxidant properties: Witch Hazel tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, leading to premature aging and discoloration, while also protecting against environmental stressors that can exacerbate oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their rich content of salicylic acid, which helps to unclog pores and reduce inflammation associated with oily skin.
Buds: The buds are used for their astringent properties, helping to shrink the appearance of pores and reducing oil production on the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves and twigs in early morning or evening to ensure peak potency.
Clean the harvested material by rinsing with cool water and gently pat dry with paper towels.
Chop the cleaned witch hazel material into small pieces and combine 30 grams with 150 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a dark place at room temperature for six to eight weeks, shaking daily during the first two weeks.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture as 1:3 or 30% alcohol.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with oily face because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to balance the skin's natural oils. The tannins in lemon balm also have an ability to reduce inflammation and calm redness, making it an excellent remedy for acne-prone skin. Additionally, its refreshing and soothing properties help to shrink pores, reducing their appearance and leaving the skin looking smoother and more even-toned.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and antibacterial properties: Lemon balm's antiseptic and antibacterial compounds can help reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin, preventing breakouts and inflammation associated with oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling caused by excess oil production, making it an effective natural treatment for acne-prone skin.

Antioxidant and astringent properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, while its astringent properties can help balance sebum production, reducing the appearance of pores and tightening the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemon balm roots help to soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Leaves: High in astringent properties, lemon balm leaves constrict pores and reduce excess oil production, making them an effective ingredient for oily face tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried, selecting fresh and fragrant material for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat or air drying method, taking 24 hours to complete.
Combine one part dried melissa officinalis leaves with one part high-proof vodka in a glass jar, filling the jar almost to the top.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the mixture every other day to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tinctures helps with oily face because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to balance the skin's natural oil production. The tannins present in rosemary help to tighten pores, reducing their appearance and minimizing the likelihood of clogged pores. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can contribute to skin inflammation and acne, leaving your skin looking clear and healthy.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Rosemary's antibacterial compounds, such as carnosic acid, help combat acne-causing bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing clogged pores.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, like rosmarinic acid, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with oily skin conditions like acne and rosacea.

Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants, including polyphenols and flavonoids, neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to aging, helping to restore balance and hydration to the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm oily skin.
Leaves: Known for their astringent properties, which help to reduce oil production and tighten pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosemary leaves from sustainable sources in a 1:1 ratio by weight with vodka at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Clean and dry the harvested rosemary leaves then chop them into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Transfer the chopped rosemary to a clean glass jar and cover it with the prepared vodka mixture ensuring all plant material is submerged for 30 days.
Store the mixture in a dark cupboard away from direct sunlight and heat then shake the jar every 2-3 days during the extraction period.

After the extraction period filter the tincture using a coffee filter or cheesecloth squeezing out as much liquid as possible discarding solids and transferring to a clean glass bottle for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium tinctures helps with oily face because of its natural ability to balance skin's pH levels, reducing excess oil production. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm irritated pores, allowing them to breathe more easily. Additionally, the antiseptic properties in wild geranium tincture work to purify the skin, eliminating dirt and impurities that can clog pores and exacerbate oiliness. Overall, using a herbal wild geranium tincture as part of your skincare routine can help to regulate sebum production and leave your complexion looking clearer and more even-toned.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic properties: Wild geranium tincture has antiseptic properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth on the skin, which can contribute to oily skin issues.
Astringent properties: The astringent properties of wild geranium tincture help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production, leading to a more balanced and less oily complexion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild geranium tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne and other skin conditions that can contribute to oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
Barks: The barks of the wild geranium plant contain astringent properties that help tighten pores and reduce excess oil production, making them an effective ingredient for controlling oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried geranium maculatum leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried ensuring high quality plant material.
Chop 20 grams of harvested plant material into small pieces and combine it with 200 milliliters of high proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a dark cool place for four to six weeks shaking daily to allow for optimal extraction and infusion process.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.
Transfer 20 milliliters of the final tincture to a dark amber glass bottle with dropper cap labeling it as 1:3 geranium maculatum tincture for further use and storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with oily face because of its natural astringent properties, which help to dry out excess sebum and reduce shine. The herb's antiseptic properties also work to combat bacteria that can contribute to acne and inflammation on the skin. Additionally, wormwood has been shown to regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to oiliness, making it an effective natural remedy for achieving a clearer, more balanced complexion.

Astringent and drying properties: The astringent and drying properties of wormwood tincture help tighten pores, absorb excess oil, and reduce sebum production, leaving the skin feeling tighter and more balanced.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties: Wormwood's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to premature aging, acne, and other skin issues, promoting overall healthy and radiant skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots are often used in herbal medicine due to their ability to absorb excess oil and toxins from the skin, making them beneficial for balancing oily complexions.
Leaves: The leaves of the wormwood plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated skin while regulating sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality artemisia absinthium leaves and stems.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm and dark place.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces using scissors.
Combine chopped plant material with 80% ethanol or vodka as solvent.
Strain and filter the tincture using cheesecloth or coffee filters.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with oily face because it has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce acne and soothe irritated skin. The tannins in pot marigold also help to constrict pores, reducing their appearance and allowing for better oil regulation. Additionally, the herbal extract has antibacterial properties that combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that can contribute to oily skin. By using pot marigold tinctures topically, you can help to balance your skin's natural oil production and achieve a clearer complexion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin conditions like acne and rosacea.

Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold's antimicrobial activity helps combat P. acnes bacteria, a common cause of acne and oily skin, by inhibiting its growth and preventing it from producing excess sebum.

Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are known for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can help to control acne and reduce inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in their full bloom for maximum potency and freshness.
Dry the fresh flowers using a dehydrator at 30°c for 24 hours or air-drying method for 7-10 days.
Combine one part dried calendula flowers with two parts high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and shake the jar daily for 2 weeks, allowing it to steep and infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture with date and ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with oily face because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds that help to reduce sebum production, unclog pores, and soothe redness and irritation. As a result, it can effectively balance the skin's natural oil production, reducing the appearance of shine and excess oil on the face. Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins and polyphenols present in purple coneflower have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin conditions like acne and rosacea.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with oily faces.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and nutrients, seeds nourish and balance the skin's natural oil production, regulating sebum levels and promoting a healthy complexion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and dry them thoroughly in the shade for at least two weeks.
Combine 100 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow for infusion and extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture liquid.
Transfer the resulting herbal purple coneflower tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, labeling them with dates and ingredients for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with oily face because of its natural ability to balance skin's oil production. The herb contains inulin, a prebiotic that feeds good bacteria on the skin, promoting a healthy gut-skin axis. This leads to reduced sebum production and a more even skin tone. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe redness and acne, leaving the skin looking clear and radiant. By incorporating dandelion tincture into your skincare routine, you can say goodbye to oily patches and hello to a balanced, glowing complexion.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion tincture helps to reduce oil production by increasing urine production, which in turn helps to flush out excess toxins and hormones that can contribute to acne and oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in dandelion tincture help to reduce inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to clogged pores and blackheads that often accompany oily skin.

Lymphatic stimulating properties: Dandelion tincture stimulates the lymphatic system, which helps to remove toxins and excess fluids from the body, including those that may be contributing to acne and oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bitter compounds that help to balance and regulate sebum production, making them effective at reducing oiliness.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm irritated skin prone to oily conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion leaves and flowers at dawn, ensuring a minimum of 2 cups of plant material.
Chop the harvested plant material into smaller pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 2 cups of chopped dandelion with 4 cups of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, sealing tightly.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily during this period, and then strain through cheesecloth.
Transfer the filtered liquid into dark glass bottles, labeling each bottle with date and dosage instructions, storing at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with oily face because they contain powerful antioxidants that help to regulate the production of sebum, the natural oil produced by the skin. The tannins and anthraquinones present in aloe vera work together to reduce inflammation and prevent pores from becoming clogged, allowing the skin to breathe freely. This leads to a balanced skin tone and reduced appearance of shine, leaving the skin looking smooth and refreshed. Regular use of herbal aloe tinctures can also help to calm acne-prone skin and reduce blackheads.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Aloe tincture helps to balance and tighten pores, reducing excess oil production and absorption, which can lead to a smoother, less shiny complexion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help to soothe and calm redness and irritation caused by oily skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a more even-toned complexion.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in aloe tincture, including vitamins C and E, help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to oiliness, promoting healthy skin cell growth and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in aloin and other anti-inflammatory compounds, aloe leaves help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily facial skin.

Roots: With their high concentration of saponins and flavonoids, aloe roots provide natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against acne-causing bacteria and fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves taking care not to damage the plant.
Blend 500g of harvested aloe vera gel with 250ml of vodka and 50ml of glycerin.
Transfer the mixture to a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding solids.
Dilute the resulting tincture with distilled water to create a 1:3 ratio of aloe vera to water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with oily face because of its natural astringent properties, which help to balance the skin's natural oil production. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems with sharp scissors or pruning shears carefully cutting at nodes.
Weigh out approximately 250 grams of dried urtica dioica material for proper ratio in preparation process.
Combine the measured urtica dioica material with high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar ensuring all plant parts are completely submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least four to six weeks shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids and storing tincture in airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon grass tinctures helps with oily face because of its natural astringent properties, which help to balance the skin's natural oils. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon schoenanthus leaves and stems in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or air-drying method.
Combine 200g of dried cymbopogon schoenanthus with 600ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to ensure extraction.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning or evening when the essential oils are most concentrated.
Weigh and chop 500 grams of harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves into small pieces for optimal extraction.
Combine the chopped leaves with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete leaf coverage.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for eight hours or overnight, allowing for efficient solvent extraction.

Chop the corn cobs into small pieces and combine with 50% vodka in a clean glass jar.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and fiber, roots help to absorb excess oil and purify the skin, reducing acne and blemishes.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of taraxacum kok-saghyz and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine 100 grams with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily for two minutes each morning.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding the solids after one hour.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with oily face because it possesses antiseptic and astringent properties that help to reduce the production of sebum, thereby controlling oiliness. Its antimicrobial qualities also inhibit the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to acne and clogged pores. Additionally, St John's Wort has anti-inflammatory effects, which soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin. As a result, using a herbal St John's Wort tincture can lead to a balanced and healthy complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: St John's Wort's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals, which can cause oxidative stress and inflammation that lead to acne and oily skin.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with oily skin, promoting a clearer and more balanced complexion.

Antiseptic and Antibacterial Properties: St John's Wort's antiseptic and antibacterial properties help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can clog pores and contribute to oily skin, reducing the risk of acne and breakouts.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, roots help to soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, flowers aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin tone.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort leaves and flowers in full sun for maximum potency and oil-absorbing properties.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 90°f for 12 hours to preserve quality.
Combine one part dried hypericum perforatum with two parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for eight weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean bottle, discarding solids, and store the finished tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with oily face because of its natural astringent properties, which help to balance the skin's oil production. The antioxidants present in fennel tincture also work to reduce inflammation and purify the pores, making it an effective remedy for reducing acne and blackheads. Additionally, fennel tincture's antiseptic properties help to kill bacteria that can cause breakouts, leaving the skin looking smoother and more radiant. As a result, using fennel tincture as a toner or spot treatment can greatly improve the overall appearance of oily skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Fennel's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin inflammation, reducing the appearance of pores and preventing further oil production.
Anti-inflammatory: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as anethole, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne and oily skin, promoting a clearer complexion.
Antimicrobial: Fennel's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which can cause clogged pores and oil production, leading to healthier, less oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are often used in herbal remedies due to their ability to soothe and calm the skin, making them ideal for oily faces.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have antiseptic properties that can help reduce acne and inflammation on the skin, making them a popular choice for oily face tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered seeds with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it daily for at least 30 seconds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid tincture.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with oily face because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal extract reduces sebum production, calming acne-prone skin and balancing oil levels. Additionally, chamomile's astringent qualities help tighten pores, minimizing the appearance of pores and reducing shine. As an natural toner, chamomile tinctures also helps to balance the skin's pH level, creating a healthy environment for skin to thrive in.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne and other skin irritations, making it an effective remedy for calming down oily skin.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of chamomile tincture can help combat bacteria that contribute to oily skin, reducing the growth of pimple-causing bacteria and preventing clogged pores.

Soothing and cooling: Chamomile's soothing and cooling effects can help calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and ease itching associated with acne, making it a gentle and non-irritating treatment for oily face.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming acne-prone skin.
Flowers: The delicate flowers of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and promote a balanced skin pH, making it ideal for oily face care.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers in full bloom for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers by gently blowing off any excess moisture and storing them in an airtight container.
Mix 250 grams of dried flowers with 500 milliliters of vodka or ethanol-based solvent in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction, before straining the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Filter the resulting tincture into dark glass bottles using a fine-mesh sieve and store it in a cool, dark place.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for oily face are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, and botanicals that help to balance and regulate the skin's natural oil production. These tinctures work by reducing sebum production, calming inflammation, and tightening pores, resulting in a clearer and more radiant complexion. Examples of herbal tinctures that can benefit an oily face include tea tree oil, chamomile, and lavender, which can be used alone or combined with other ingredients to create a customized skincare blend. By using these tinctures, individuals with oily skin can enjoy a reduced appearance of pores, less acne, and a more even-toned complexion, leading to increased confidence and a healthier-looking glow.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily face with herbal tinctures is to focus on herbs that help regulate oil production and balance skin pH. Try using a blend of tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile tincture mixed with witch hazel or rosewater as a toner. Apply it to the face twice daily to reduce sebum output and soothe acne-prone skin. Additionally, take internal supplements like turmeric, dandelion root, and nettle leaf to address hormonal imbalances that contribute to oily skin.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily face that are treated with herbal tinctures are acne, eczema, and psoriasis. These conditions involve skin inflammation, excess oil production, or dryness, which can be effectively managed with herbal remedies. Herbal tinctures containing ingredients like tea tree oil, lavender, and aloe vera have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness, itching, and irritation.
